name: HUC-JIR Year-in-Israel
organization: Hebrew Union College - Jewish Institute of Religion
goodFor: For first-year HUC-JIR rabbinical, cantorial and Jewish-education graduate students (enrollment is automatic upon admission, not separately applied for) wanting a required, immersive year that uses Israel itself as the classroom.

name: JTS Israel Year
organization: Jewish Theological Seminary, at the Schechter Institute of Jewish Studies
goodFor: For JTS rabbinical students (required as part of the degree, not separately applied for) wanting a year of advanced Jewish text study at the Schechter Institute alongside deep engagement with Israeli society and history.

name: Ziegler School Israel Summer Program
organization: American Jewish University, Ziegler School of Rabbinic Studies
goodFor: For Ziegler rabbinical students (required, not separately applied for) wanting an intensive 10-12 week Israel immersion as part of their Conservative ordination track.
note: Public sources don't name a single host campus (just 'Israel') since the program was recently restructured from a full year to a 10-12 week intensive — travelType left blank.

name: RRC Israel Summer Term
durationText: 1 summer term, with an optional extension to a full year
goodFor: For Reconstructionist Rabbinical College students (required, not separately applied for) wanting a summer term of Israel immersion, with the option to extend to a full year via RRC scholarships.
note: Public sources don't name a single host campus (just 'Israel') — travelType left blank.

name: Hashomer Hatzair Shnat Hachshara
goodFor: For members of the secular, Labor-Zionist Hashomer Hatzair youth movement (active in a dozen-plus countries) wanting a 10-month communal-living gap year in Israel before returning home to lead the movement.
note: No single host campus named in public sources (movement runs it across multiple kibbutzim/sites) — travelType left blank.

name: Shnat Betar
location: Kiryat Moriah (Jerusalem) and sites across Israel
goodFor: For Revisionist-Zionist Betar movement members wanting an 8-month gap year mixing leadership training alongside 100+ peers from other youth movements worldwide, a taste of IDF service, and touring in Israel and Poland.

name: Limmud (World Bnei Akiva)
goodFor: For religious-Zionist gap-year students specifically from Australia, New Zealand, South Africa or South America wanting Torah study paired with self-empowerment and personal-growth programming.

name: Shvilim Traveling Gap Year Program
organization: TUGLA, hosted at the Upper Galilee Leadership Academy (Mechinat Galil Elyon)
location: Upper Galilee, Israel, with travel to Oslo and Jewish communities worldwide
goodFor: For 18-22-year-old European, Israeli and American Jews of any observance level wanting a 10-month program blending Israel immersion, University of Oslo coursework, and travel to Jewish communities worldwide.

name: CAMERA Fellows - Student Leadership and Advocacy Mission to Israel
location: Jerusalem, Tel Aviv, and a kibbutz on the Kinneret
goodFor: For CAMERA Fellows and campus Israel-advocacy activists (US, Canada, Uruguay) wanting an intensive 2-week Israel mission of expert speakers and touring to sharpen their advocacy skills before returning to campus.

name: Vertigo International Dance Program (VIDP)
location: Vertigo Eco-Art Village, Kibbutz Netiv HaLamed-Heh (with related programs in Jerusalem)
durationText: Varies by program (VIDP main program, VDC Prep-Workshop)
goodFor: For trained contemporary dancers (men 18-28, women 18-25) from Israel and worldwide wanting professional-level training at a kibbutz-based dance company recognized by Israel's Ministry of Culture & Sports.
